channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump and Musk aren't the first to make deep cuts. Clinton-era Reinventing Government saved billions
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A new administration swept into Washington and announced plans to shake it up, using corporate know-how and new technology to streamline the federal bureaucracy. It offered millions of government employees buyouts and slashed costs to balance the budget.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump goes on ‘unprecedented’ Pentagon firing spree: report
item.description: President Donald Trump went on an "unprecedented" shakeup of the Pentagon over the weekend, firing the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff and several other top officers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Key agencies, including some led by Trump loyalists, refuse to comply with Musk's latest demand
item.description: Key federal agencies from the FBI and State Department to the Pentagon have instructed their employees not to comply with Elon Musk’s demand that they explain what they accomplished last week or risk losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump directs ICE agents to find, deport unaccompanied migrant children
item.description: More than 600,000 immigrant children have crossed the U.S.-Mexico border without a parent or legal guardian since 2019.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Illinois bill would create state's own DOGE to eliminate wasteful spending
item.description: Illinois is one of more than a dozen states suing the Trump administration over the federal DOGE agency.
item.dc:creator: John Clark, NewsNation

item.title: The Memo: Trump’s jabs at Ukraine divide GOP
item.description: President Trump ratcheted up his attacks on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday, deepening the first real schism of his second term within the GOP.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fired employees fear beloved Yosemite National Park will lose its luster
item.description: Yosemite National Park employees who recently were fired by the U.S. government worry cuts will affect the experience of visitors and the welfare of wildlife that thrive in the popular vacation destination located in California’s Sierra Nevada.
item.dc:creator: Haven Daley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump fires top US general in unprecedented Pentagon shakeup
item.description: A U.S. official said Brown was relieved with immediate effect, even before the Senate confirms his successor.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: U.S. Department of Agriculture suspends scholarship program at NC A&T State University, 18 other historically Black colleges, website shows
item.description: GREENSBORO, N.C. (WGHP) — The U.S. Department of Agriculture has suspended a scholarship program that serves students from rural or underserved communities pending further review, according to its website.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, NewsNation

item.title: Union workers written up, fired for using sick days; Nevada lawmaker moves to close loophole
item.description: Some union workers are afraid to use sick days because they might be written up or fired for being "undependable," and now a Nevada lawmaker is coming to their aid.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California lawmakers advocate for dual language immersion programs with new bill
item.description: Assemblymember Mark González is introducing AB 865, a bill that would grant the Dual Language Immersion program $5 million to increase access to instructional materials in partner languages, with the goal of educational equity and multilingual proficiency.
item.dc:creator: Adoreil Ayoubgoulan, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department files complaint against judge weighing challenge to Trump’s transgender troop ban
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department filed a complaint Friday accusing a federal judge in Washington of misconduct during hearings over President Donald Trump's executive order that calls for banning transgender troops from serving in the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DeSantis reappoints Moms for Liberty cofounder to Florida ethics commission
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis is attempting to put Moms for Liberty cofounder Tina Descovich on the Florida Commission on Ethics for the second time.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Planned medical research grant cuts worry Sarasota scientists
item.description: For more than 20 years, Sarasota-based biomedical research facility The Roskamp Institute has been focused on understanding and finding treatment, prevention, and early detection for Alzheimer's disease and other dementia, along with illnesses facing veterans.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cortez Masto, Rosen introduce proposal to block Trump plan to end birthright citizenship
item.description: Nevada Democratic Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto and Jacky Rosen introduced a proposal Thursday to block President Donald Trump's attempt to end the constitutional right to birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Kluwe arrested while protesting MAGA sign at city council meeting
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe was detained at a city council meeting in California on Tuesday after delivering an impassioned speech criticizing the design of a plaque to be installed at the Huntington Beach Central Library.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Sen. Mitch McConnell won't seek reelection in 2026, ending long tenure as Republican power broker
item.description: Fellow senators have clapped for Mitch McConnell after he announced he won’t seek reelection next year. The Kentucky Republican is ending a decadeslong tenure as a power broker who championed conservative causes but ultimately ceded ground to the fierce populism of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Kennedy Center cancels Pride performance featuring Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington
item.description: The chorus, one of the oldest LGBTQ choral organizations in the U.S., was set to perform alongside the National Symphony Orchestra in May.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Trump may buy used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: It's not the planes, rather the heavy modification to make them suitable for the requirements of presidential travel and the top-level security clearances required for those involved, that has added to the cost and delays.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A gold-framed Trump mug shot is hanging just outside the Oval Office
item.description: President Trump’s mug shot is one of the first things visitors will see when approaching the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise, MPR News

item.title: Gov. Gavin Newsom proposes $125M mortgage relief program to help victims of recent natural disasters
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom on Wednesday proposed a new $125 million package to help residents whose homes were destroyed or damaged by recent natural disasters, including the recent wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Democrat reintroduces 'fake electors' bill following governor's veto
item.description: Nevada lawmakers will once again debate a bill to penalize a person who creates a false slate of presidential electors -- except this time with lesser penalties.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FAA firings begin weeks after deadly crash near DC
item.description: The Trump administration has begun firing hundreds of employees at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) just weeks after a deadly crash outside of Washington, D.C., put a spotlight on the agency.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration tries to bring back fired nuclear weapons workers, reversing DOGE cuts
item.description: he Trump administration has halted the firings of hundreds of federal employees who were tasked with working on the nation's nuclear weapons programs, in an about-face that has left workers confused and experts cautioning that DOGE's blind cost cutting will put communities at risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Washington Post backs out of 'Fire Elon Musk' ad order
item.description: The Washington Post this week backed out of a “Fire Elon Musk” advertising order that was to run as a wrap on some of its Tuesday editions, according to the advocacy group Common Cause.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5
